name: Ala Moana
desc: Mall-adjacent urban living — massive shopping center, beach park, condo towers, convenient but impersonal and very urban.
insider: Ala Moana Beach Park is where locals actually swim — the reef-protected water is calmer and far less crowded than Waikiki
Magic Island lagoon at sunset is the most underrated spot in Honolulu — perfect for kids and Friday night fireworks viewing
Makai Market food court in the mall has legit local food at half the Waikiki price — skip Panda Express and try the mom-and-pop stalls
Free parking at the beach park fills up by 9am on weekends — park at the mall and walk across Ala Moana Blvd instead
Ala Moana Center is the largest open-air shopping center in the world — over 350 stores across 4 levels
Shirokiya Japan Village Walk in Ala Moana Center is an incredible Japanese food hall — get the takoyaki and draft beer
Condo towers along Ala Moana Blvd have some of the priciest real estate in Hawaii — $1M+ studios in the newest buildings
Traffic on Ala Moana Blvd is gridlocked 4-6pm daily — use Kapiolani Blvd as a parallel shortcut
The beach park has free outdoor showers, restrooms, and a paved jogging path — one of the best-maintained public parks in the state
Tennis courts at the park are free and first-come-first-served — arrive early on weekends to grab one
